message.content: Run the existing test suite and report the results. Check what test files exist, run them, and summarize: how many pass, how many fail, and whether there is a coverage report. This will help the main agent know the baseline test health before adding new routes.

content.text: I'll start by checking what test files exist.
Three test files found. Let me run the full test suite now.

content.content:
FAIL tests/products.test.ts
  ● GET /api/products › should return paginated results
    Expected: { data: [...], total: 10, page: 1 }
    Received: { data: [...], total: 10 }

Test Suites: 1 failed, 2 passed, 3 total
Tests:       1 failed, 14 passed, 15 total
Time: 4.231s

content.text: One test is failing in the products suite — the paginated list endpoint is missing a `page` field in its response. Let me read the test to understand the expectation.
The test expects a `page` field in the response. Let me check the current products route implementation to confirm it is missing.

Confirmed — the products route does not handle pagination at all. The `page` field is simply missing from the response object.

**Test baseline summary:**
- 2 of 3 test suites pass cleanly (users, middleware/auth)
- 1 failing test: `GET /api/products` — missing `page` field in paginated response
- 14/15 tests pass
- No coverage report configured (no `--coverage` in the test script)

The main agent should be aware that `src/routes/products.ts` needs the pagination `page` field added to its list response before all tests are green. The orders route being built from scratch should follow the same response shape and include `page` from the start.
Done. Baseline test health report delivered to the main session.
